creator: cperciva@FreeBSD.org
text:
> I believe freebsd-update does not pull down both versions.  Instead, I
> think it "knows" on the server which binary diffs to send down to convert
> specific known old versions of files to the current version of each file.

For files in MergeChanges, the freebsd-update client will download both versions and then use diff3 to merge changes.

For other files, the freebsd-update client will either leave the existing file unchanged (UpdateIfUnmodified files with hashes which don't match the old release) or update to the new version; that updating can optionally use binary diffs but that's just an optimization and if binary diffs don't work then it will fetch the entire new file.
